spec_fidelity.notes: New parserParity.test.ts encodes a TS↔Rust output divergence (empty-string: TS Ok([]) vs Rust Ok([paragraph("")])) as accepted behavior, but verification-architecture.md §13 line 640 / behavioral-spec.md lines 716–723 explicitly require parity with the TS canonical contract AND state that the Rust non-empty invariant 'is consistent with the TS parseMarkdownToBlocks 最低 1 ブロック保持 invariant' — a factual contradiction. Either the spec is wrong about TS or the chosen $lib implementation is wrong relative to the spec. Either way the parity claim PROP-FEED-S4-016 is unproven for any input that exercises the TS PROP-031 (blank/whitespace input) branch, and the test as written documents that gap rather than closing it. New finding: FIND-S4-IMPL-iter2-001.

implementation_correctness.notes: No regressions to existing implementation paths from the new tests. prop_s4_016b correctly asserts the spec-mandated canonical fixture shape. parserParity.test.ts exercises a real exported parser and the assertions are non-trivial (compares structural type+content tuples).

perFindingStatus.FIND-S4-IMPL-001: PARTIALLY_RESOLVED — parserParity.test.ts now exists with the canonical fixture and 6-case suite, but (a) it imports $lib/domain/capture-auto-save/parse-markdown-to-blocks, NOT the canonical TS reference docs/domain/code/ts/src/shared/blocks.ts indicated by the spec authors, and (b) the test legitimizes a TS↔Rust divergence on the empty/whitespace branch instead of failing on it. The Sprint 4 gate (canonical 1-pair snapshot) is satisfied by the canonical-fixture test, but the broader parity claim it pretends to evidence is not. New finding FIND-S4-IMPL-iter2-001 records the gap.
perFindingStatus.FIND-S4-IMPL-002: NOT_RESOLVED — integration_smoke_select_past_note_handler_logic still does not invoke select_past_note. The function body re-runs scan_vault_feed → parse_markdown_to_blocks → compose_state_for_select_past_note → make_editing_state_changed_payload directly, exactly the antipattern iter-1 called out. The Sprint 5 deferral comment is plausible for the Tauri `test` feature route but FIND-S4-IMPL-002 explicitly proposed a Mock Emitter trait alternative that does not require nightly Tauri features. The builder did not attempt the Mock Emitter route. Net effect: zero handler-side regression coverage was added; only the documentation around the gap improved. New finding FIND-S4-IMPL-iter2-002 records this.
perFindingStatus.FIND-S4-IMPL-003: RESOLVED — prop_s4_016b_canonical_two_block_snapshot uses the spec fixture '# heading\n\nparagraph', asserts len==2 with [0]=Heading1/'heading' and [1]=Paragraph/'paragraph'. Pairs symmetrically with the parserParity.test.ts canonical-fixture test.

purity_boundary.notes: parserParity.test.ts imports only $lib/domain/capture-auto-save/parse-markdown-to-blocks. No @tauri-apps/api, no svelte/store, no listen/invoke. The new Rust tests use only stdlib + promptnotes_lib::editor / promptnotes_lib::feed exports. No purity-boundary regression.

wire_compatibility.notes: No new wire-shape assertions contradict existing JSON-key contracts (status / currentNoteId / focusedBlockId / blocks / pendingNextFocus). parserParity.test.ts only checks (type, content) tuples on parser output, which is a subset of the existing wire contract and does not introduce contradictions.
